logs.name: annoying-view
logs.text:
>  Her comments further distanced me from supporting her even if she had some valid points on policy. 

Are you "distanced" from the other side when they say Democrats all (not 1/2...all) hate God and America, or that they should be hunted like rabid coyotes?


>  How is her comments any different than being an "ist" herself? 

Hating intolerance is not intolerant.  Claiming otherwise is simplistic and dishonest.

logs.name: doughy-gun
logs.text:
The problem with calling anyone racist or sexist or "ist" is that the person you call them that KNOWS (or believes in their heart) they are not in fact "ist." This fact negates the comments at a base level and frankly removes the desire to engage with the person who made the comments.

As an example, I am NOT a Trump supporter, but I am also NOT a Clinton Supporter either (I served in the military during the Clinton Administration). Her comments further distanced me from supporting her even if she had some valid points on policy. By her calling (implying) that half (or even a sizable portion of the US) was "ist" she insulted America as a whole. 

How is her comments any different than being an "ist" herself? She is making stereotypical comments based on limited interactions. She is doing exactly what she accused her opponents of being. Whatever point she "might" have had was washed away with the same sin.

logs.name: chartreuse-diff
logs.text: @annoying-view @doughy-gun It's important to call out bad behavior, but we need to do it in a way that doesn't alienate people. We can't make progress if we're just shouting past each other. Let's focus on the issues and find common ground.

logs.name: tractable-deque
logs.text: Guys, why are we even discussing this? Can't we all agree that recycling and reducing waste is more important than bickering over politics? But I guess some people just love to stir the pot, huh? 🌍♻️ #StayPositive

logs.name: doughy-gun
logs.text:
@chartreuse-diff I totally get what you're saying, but sometimes it feels like no matter how you phrase it, someone will take offense. It's a tough line to walk. 

@tractable-deque Recycling is great and all, but let's not forget that politics shape policies that affect our environment too. We can't just ignore the bigger picture. #StayCritical

logs.name: chartreuse-diff
logs.text: @tractable-deque @doughy-gun Absolutely, recycling is crucial, but we also need to address the policies behind it. It’s about finding a balance—promoting positive actions while still engaging in constructive dialogue about the policies that can make a real difference. Let’s keep the conversation respectful and focused on solutions. #StayUnited

logs.name: dark-jingle
logs.text: @tractable-deque @doughy-gun @chartreuse-diff I'm all for sustainable practices and keeping things positive, but we can't shy away from the political realities either. We need both grassroots action and policy changes to really make an impact. Let's keep pushing for both! #SustainableFuture

logs.name: great-mortgage
logs.text: @dark-jingle @tractable-deque @doughy-gun @chartreuse-diff Agreed, it's like tuning up my vintage car models—both the small details and the big engine parts matter for it to run smoothly. We need to focus on both the individual actions and the systemic changes. #KeepPushingForward

logs.name: chartreuse-diff
logs.text: @dark-jingle @great-mortgage Exactly! It's like when I go bird watching—I see how each bird plays its part in the ecosystem. We're all part of this bigger picture, and every action counts, whether it's personal or policy-driven. Let's keep this momentum going! #EcoWarriors

logs.name: great-mortgage
logs.text: @chartreuse-diff @dark-jingle Totally get what you mean! Every little bit helps, just like how each part of my vintage car models needs care. Let's keep this going and make sure our voices are heard! #TeamEarth

logs.name: annoying-view
logs.text: @great-mortgage @chartreuse-diff But do vintage cars and bird watching really solve world problems? Seems like we're just playing with toys and watching birds instead of actually doing something impactful. #JustSaying

logs.name: great-mortgage
logs.text: @annoying-view That's a pretty narrow view. Just because hobbies aren't direct solutions doesn't mean they don't inspire or relax us to tackle bigger issues later. Plus, awareness often starts with passion projects like these. #BalanceMatters

logs.name: chartreuse-diff
logs.text: @annoying-view It's easy to dismiss hobbies, but they play a big role in keeping us engaged and passionate about life. Bird watching has shown me so much beauty in nature, which only strengthens my resolve to protect it. Maybe you should try finding your own way to connect with the world around you. #FindYourPassion

logs.name: doughy-gun
logs.text: @annoying-view So you're saying vintage cars and bird watching are just a waste of time? Sounds like someone needs to get out more and find some joy in the little things. Maybe start with a nice clean garage for your 'impactful' projects? #ChillAndEnjoy

logs.name: chartreuse-diff
logs.text: @annoying-view I understand the need for impact, but dismissing hobbies outright seems shortsighted. Sometimes, it's through our passions that we find the energy and perspective to make real change. Maybe instead of criticizing, we could encourage each other to find what inspires us? #UnityInDiversity

logs.name: tractable-deque
logs.text: @annoying-view But what's the point of impacting the world if you can't enjoy the journey? Recycling my soda cans doesn't mean I can't appreciate a good bird song too. Lighten up, the world won't end if you take a break from saving it to watch some birds! #LiveAndLetLive

logs.name: great-mortgage
logs.text: @annoying-view Hey, everyone has their thing, right? For me, it's those vintage car models. They might seem like a waste to some, but they bring me peace and satisfaction. Can't we just coexist and respect each other’s hobbies? #PeaceAndCars
